title: 修复跨平台 Make 输出测试兼容性

sources.reason: Failing CI test and cross-platform Make output assertion.

riskAssessment.reason: GNU Make versions differ in recipe indentation when dry-running shell conditionals; the test must assert branch semantics, not whitespace.

executionDecision.reason: The CI failure and intended test semantics are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Ensure the test remains read-only and does not weaken no-active Work Item checks.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The no-active branch test identifies the else branch across supported Make output indentation styles.
The test still asserts no status mutation and retains the PR gate requirement.
The focused test and complete project quality suite pass.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: GitHub Actions failed because tests/test_makefile.py assumed GNU Make would render the shell else branch with a tab, while the runner emitted different whitespace.
constraints: Do not change check-ai behavior or remove no-active branch assertions.
Keep the test portable across Make implementations and read-only.
intent.rationale: Matching the semantic else branch instead of exact indentation removes a CI-only false failure without weakening coverage.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The test passes when Make emits tab-indented else output.
The test passes when Make emits space-indented else output.
No-active branch assertions still reject status mutation and require the PR gate.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
